subject: Career Education
topic: Careers 8: identity, strengths, and career pathways

classroom_overview.title: Careers 8 Launch Organizer
classroom_overview.subtitle: Strengths, pathways, and questions
classroom_overview.prompt: Start with what you know about yourself. List possible pathways, then write questions that could guide your research or a conversation.
success_criteria: I named specific strengths or interests.
I listed pathways without deciding too quickly.
I wrote questions that could guide my next step.

strengths_inventory.title: Careers 8 Strengths Inventory
strengths_inventory.subtitle: Use real examples to name how you contribute
strengths_inventory.learning_target: I can name strengths and connect them to possible pathways.
strengths_inventory.directions: Choose three strengths. For each one, give a real example from school, home, sports, work, volunteering, or community life.
strengths_inventory.prompt: A strength is more useful when you can prove it. Explain what you did, what helped, and where that strength might matter later.

vocabulary.definition: a skill, habit, or quality that helps you contribute
a real example that proves your idea
a possible direction for learning, work, or service

success_criteria: I gave a specific example for each strength.
I connected at least one strength to a pathway.
My writing sounds specific to me.

career_cluster_cer.title: Career Cluster CER
career_cluster_cer.subtitle: Choose a pathway and support your thinking
career_cluster_cer.question: Which career pathway is worth exploring next, and why?
career_cluster_cer.learning_target: I can support a career-learning claim with evidence about myself.
career_cluster_cer.directions: Pick one pathway. Use evidence from your strengths, interests, and questions. Explain why this pathway is worth another look.
sentence_starters: A pathway I want to explore is...
One question I still have is...
This pathway is worth exploring because...
success_criteria: My claim names a specific pathway.
My evidence includes something true about me.
I included one useful research question.

career_interview_organizer.title: Career Interview Question Planner
career_interview_organizer.subtitle: Prepare questions that get useful answers
career_interview_organizer.prompt: Write questions for an adult, guest speaker, family member, community member, video profile, or career article.
columns: What this could help me learn
success_criteria: I ask about daily work, training, challenges, and advice.
At least one question connects to my strengths or interests.

next_step_reflection.title: Careers 8 Exit Reflection
next_step_reflection.subtitle: Name one learning and one next step
next_step_reflection.learning_target: I can reflect on career learning and choose a realistic next step.
next_step_reflection.prompt: What did you learn about yourself or a pathway today? What will you do next?
success_criteria: I named one thing I learned.
I named one specific next step.
My next step is realistic for Grade 8.
